inputFields.name: rounds
inputFields.description: Rounds for hash calculation. Used by scrypt and similar algorithms.

inputFields.description: The key for to hash the password.

inputFields.description: Whether allow overwrite existing account when user local_id exists.

inputFields.description: The following 4 fields are for standard scrypt algorithm.

inputFields.description: The password hash algorithm.

inputFields.name: saltSeparator
inputFields.description: The salt separator.

inputFields.name: sanityCheck
inputFields.description: If true, backend will do sanity check(including duplicate email and federated id) when uploading account.

inputFields.name: targetProjectId
inputFields.description: Specify which project (field value is actually project id) to operate. Only used when provided credential.

inputFields.name: memoryCost
inputFields.description: Memory cost for hash calculation. Used by scrypt similar algorithms.

inputFields.name: delegatedProjectNumber
inputFields.description: GCP project number of the requesting delegated app. Currently only intended for Firebase V1 migration.

inputFields.name: users
inputFields.description: The account info to be stored.

types.name: CreateAuthUriResponse
types.description: Response of creating the IDP authentication URL.

fields.name: allProviders
fields.description: all providers the user has once used to do federated login

fields.name: registered
fields.description: Whether the user is registered if the identifier is an email.

fields.name: captchaRequired
fields.description: True if captcha is required.

fields.name: forExistingProvider
fields.description: True if the authUri is for user's existing provider.

fields.name: sessionId
fields.description: Session ID which should be passed in the following verifyAssertion request.

fields.name: signinMethods
fields.description: All sign-in methods this user has used.

fields.name: providerId
fields.description: The provider ID of the auth URI.

fields.name: authUri
fields.description: The URI used by the IDP to authenticate the user.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The fixed string identitytoolkit#CreateAuthUriResponse".

types.name: IdentitytoolkitRelyingpartyCreateAuthUriRequest_Input
types.description: Request to get the IDP authentication URL.

inputFields.name: continueUri
inputFields.description: The URI to which the IDP redirects the user after the federated login flow.

inputFields.name: customParameter
inputFields.description: The query parameter that client can customize by themselves in auth url. The following parameters are reserved for server so that they cannot be customized by clients: client_id, response_type, scope, redirect_uri, state, oauth_token.

inputFields.name: providerId
inputFields.description: The IdP ID. For white listed IdPs it's a short domain name e.g. google.com, aol.com, live.net and yahoo.com. For other OpenID IdPs it's the OP identifier.

inputFields.name: openidRealm
inputFields.description: Optional realm for OpenID protocol. The sub string "scheme://domain:port" of the param "continueUri" is used if this is not set.

inputFields.name: otaApp
inputFields.description: The native app package for OTA installation.

inputFields.name: oauthScope
inputFields.description: Additional oauth scopes, beyond the basid user profile, that the user would be prompted to grant

inputFields.name: context
inputFields.description: The opaque value used by the client to maintain context info between the authentication request and the IDP callback.

inputFields.name: appId
inputFields.description: The app ID of the mobile app, base64(CERT_SHA1):PACKAGE_NAME for Android, BUNDLE_ID for iOS.

inputFields.name: oauthConsumerKey
inputFields.description: The developer's consumer key for OpenId OAuth Extension

inputFields.name: hostedDomain
inputFields.description: The hosted domain to restrict sign-in to accounts at that domain for Google Apps hosted accounts.

inputFields.name: tenantProjectNumber
inputFields.description: Tenant project number to be used for idp discovery.

inputFields.name: clientId
inputFields.description: The relying party OAuth client ID.

inputFields.name: identifier
inputFields.description: The email or federated ID of the user.

inputFields.name: authFlowType
inputFields.description: Explicitly specify the auth flow type. Currently only support "CODE_FLOW" type. The field is only used for Google provider.

inputFields.name: sessionId
inputFields.description: The session_id passed by client.

inputFields.name: tenantId
inputFields.description: For multi-tenant use cases, in order to construct sign-in URL with the correct IDP parameters, Firebear needs to know which Tenant to retrieve IDP configs from.

fields.name: oauthRequestToken
fields.description: The user approved request token for the OpenID OAuth extension.

fields.name: localId
fields.description: The RP local ID if it's already been mapped to the IdP account identified by the federated ID.

fields.name: verifiedProvider
fields.description: When action is 'map', contains the idps which can be used for confirmation.

fields.name: oauthAccessToken
fields.description: The OAuth2 access token.

fields.name: providerId
fields.description: The IdP ID. For white listed IdPs it's a short domain name e.g. google.com, aol.com, live.net and yahoo.com. If the "providerId" param is set to OpenID OP identifer other than the whilte listed IdPs the OP identifier is returned. If the "identifier" param is federated ID in the createAuthUri request. The domain part of the federated ID is returned.

fields.name: needConfirmation
fields.description: Whether the assertion is from a non-trusted IDP and need account linking confirmation.

fields.name: oauthExpireIn
fields.description: The lifetime in seconds of the OAuth2 access token.

fields.name: isNewUser
fields.description: True if it's a new user sign-in, false if it's a returning user.

fields.name: appInstallationUrl
fields.description: URL for OTA app installation.

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.description: The birth date of the IdP account.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The email returned by the IdP. NOTE: The federated login user may not own the email.

fields.name: nickName
fields.description: The nick name of the user.

fields.name: originalEmail
fields.description: The original email stored in the mapping storage. It's returned when the federated ID is associated to a different email.

fields.name: refreshToken
fields.description: If idToken is STS id token, then this field will be refresh token.

fields.name: rawUserInfo
fields.description: Raw IDP-returned user info.

fields.name: firstName
fields.description: The first name of the user.

fields.name: emailRecycled
fields.description: It's true if the email is recycled.

fields.name: errorMessage
fields.description: Client error code.

fields.name: fullName
fields.description: The full name of the user.

fields.name: language
fields.description: The language preference of the user.

fields.name: inputEmail
fields.description: It's the identifier param in the createAuthUri request if the identifier is an email. It can be used to check whether the user input email is different from the asserted email.

fields.name: lastName
fields.description: The last name of the user.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The display name of the user.

fields.name: action
fields.description: The action code.

fields.name: oauthIdToken
fields.description: The OIDC id token.

fields.name: appScheme
fields.description: The custom scheme used by mobile app.

fields.name: timeZone
fields.description: The timezone of the user.

fields.name: photoUrl
fields.description: The URI of the public accessible profiel picture.

fields.name: expiresIn
fields.description: If idToken is STS id token, then this field will be expiration time of STS id token in seconds.

fields.name: screenName
fields.description: The screen_name of a Twitter user or the login name at Github.

fields.name: context
fields.description: The opaque value used by the client to maintain context info between the authentication request and the IDP callback.

fields.name: federatedId
fields.description: The unique ID identifies the IdP account.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The fixed string "identitytoolkit#VerifyAssertionResponse".

fields.name: oauthTokenSecret
fields.description: The OAuth1 access token secret.

fields.name: oauthScope
fields.description: The scope for the OpenID OAuth extension.

types.name: IdentitytoolkitRelyingpartyVerifyAssertionRequest_Input
types.description: Request to verify the IDP assertion.

inputFields.name: sessionId
inputFields.description: Session ID, which should match the one in previous createAuthUri request.

inputFields.name: returnIdpCredential
inputFields.description: Whether return 200 and IDP credential rather than throw exception when federated id is already linked.

inputFields.name: tenantProjectNumber
inputFields.description: Tenant project number to be used for idp discovery.

inputFields.name: idToken
inputFields.description: The GITKit token of the authenticated user.

inputFields.name: requestUri
inputFields.description: The URI to which the IDP redirects the user back. It may contain federated login result params added by the IDP.

inputFields.name: pendingIdToken
inputFields.description: The GITKit token for the non-trusted IDP pending to be confirmed by the user.

inputFields.name: tenantId
inputFields.description: For multi-tenant use cases, in order to construct sign-in URL with the correct IDP parameters, Firebear needs to know which Tenant to retrieve IDP configs from.

inputFields.name: returnSecureToken
inputFields.description: Whether return sts id token and refresh token instead of gitkit token.

inputFields.name: postBody
inputFields.description: The post body if the request is a HTTP POST.

inputFields.name: returnRefreshToken
inputFields.description: Whether to return refresh tokens.

inputFields.name: instanceId
inputFields.description: Instance id token of the app.

inputFields.name: autoCreate
inputFields.description: When it's true, automatically creates a new account if the user doesn't exist. When it's false, allows existing user to sign in normally and throws exception if the user doesn't exist.

inputFields.name: delegatedProjectNumber
inputFields.description: GCP project number of the requesting delegated app. Currently only intended for Firebase V1 migration.

types.name: ResetPasswordResponse
types.description: Response of resetting the password.

fields.name: requestType
fields.description: The request type.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The user's email. If the out-of-band code is for email recovery, the user's original email.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The fixed string "identitytoolkit#ResetPasswordResponse".

fields.name: newEmail
fields.description: If the out-of-band code is for email recovery, the user's new email.

types.name: IdentitytoolkitRelyingpartyResetPasswordRequest_Input
types.description: Request to reset the password.

inputFields.name: email
inputFields.description: The email address of the user.

inputFields.name: oldPassword
inputFields.description: The old password inputted by the user.

inputFields.name: newPassword
inputFields.description: The new password inputted by the user.

inputFields.name: oobCode
inputFields.description: The confirmation code.

types.name: IdentitytoolkitRelyingpartySetProjectConfigResponse
types.description: Response of setting the project configuration.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project ID of the relying party.

types.name: IdentitytoolkitRelyingpartySetProjectConfigRequest_Input
types.description: Request to set the project configuration.

inputFields.name: idpConfig
inputFields.description: Oauth2 provider configuration.

inputFields.name: delegatedProjectNumber
inputFields.description: GCP project number of the requesting delegated app. Currently only intended for Firebase V1 migration.

inputFields.name: useEmailSending
inputFields.description: Whether to use email sending provided by Firebear.

inputFields.name: authorizedDomains
inputFields.description: Authorized domains for widget redirect.

inputFields.name: allowPasswordUser
inputFields.description: Whether to allow password user sign in or sign up.

inputFields.name: apiKey
inputFields.description: Browser API key, needed when making http request to Apiary.

inputFields.name: enableAnonymousUser
inputFields.description: Whether to enable anonymous user.

types.name: EmailTemplate_Input
types.description: Template for an email template.

inputFields.name: from
inputFields.description: From address of the email.

inputFields.name: format
inputFields.description: Email body format.

inputFields.name: fromDisplayName
inputFields.description: From display name.

inputFields.name: body
inputFields.description: Email body.

inputFields.name: replyTo
inputFields.description: Reply-to address.

inputFields.name: subject
inputFields.description: Subject of the email.

types.name: IdpConfig_Input
types.description: Template for a single idp configuration.

inputFields.name: whitelistedAudiences
inputFields.description: Whitelisted client IDs for audience check.

inputFields.name: experimentPercent
inputFields.description: Percent of users who will be prompted/redirected federated login for this IDP.

inputFields.name: enabled
inputFields.description: Whether this IDP is enabled.

inputFields.name: provider
inputFields.description: OAuth2 provider.

inputFields.name: clientId
inputFields.description: OAuth2 client ID.

inputFields.name: secret
inputFields.description: OAuth2 client secret.

types.name: SignupNewUserResponse
types.description: Response of signing up new user, creating anonymous user or anonymous user reauth.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The fixed string "identitytoolkit#SignupNewUserResponse".

fields.description: If idToken is STS id token, then this field will be expiration time of STS id token in seconds.

fields.name: refreshToken
fields.description: If idToken is STS id token, then this field will be refresh token.

fields.name: email
fields.description: The email of the user.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The name of the user.

fields.name: idToken
fields.description: The Gitkit id token to login the newly sign up user.

fields.name: localId
fields.description: The RP local ID of the user.

inputFields.name: displayName

inputFields.name: captchaChallenge

inputFields.name: instanceId
inputFields.description: Instance id token of the app.

inputFields.name: photoUrl
inputFields.description: The photo url of the user.

inputFields.name: disabled
inputFields.description: Whether to disable the user. Only can be used by service account.

inputFields.name: tenantProjectNumber

inputFields.description: Mark the email as verified or not. Only can be used by service account.

inputFields.description: The GITKit token of the authenticated user.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
